subject: VPN connectivity issue from home office
description:
Hi IT Support Team,

I hope this email finds you well. I wanted to reach out regarding an issue I have been experiencing with our corporate VPN connection. I understand you are all very busy and I appreciate your time in looking into this matter for me.

So, to provide some background, I have been working remotely from my home office for the past three years now, ever since the company transitioned to a hybrid work model back in 2023. During that time, I have generally had a good experience with the VPN, and I want to acknowledge the hard work that the IT team does to keep everything running smoothly.

However, starting approximately two weeks ago, I began noticing intermittent disconnections from the VPN. At first, I thought it might be related to my home internet service provider, so I contacted them and they ran diagnostics on my line. They confirmed that my connection is stable and there are no issues on their end. I also tested my internet speed using speedtest.net and I am getting consistent download speeds of approximately 200 Mbps and upload speeds of approximately 50 Mbps, which should be more than sufficient for VPN usage.

After ruling out my ISP, I tried several troubleshooting steps on my own. First, I restarted my laptop, which is a Dell Latitude 5540 running Windows 11 Enterprise, build 22631.3880. I also tried restarting my home router, which is a Netgear Nighthawk R7000. I updated the router firmware to the latest version available on the Netgear support site. I also tried connecting directly via an Ethernet cable instead of WiFi, but the VPN disconnections still occurred.

The disconnections seem to happen randomly throughout the day, but I have noticed they occur more frequently during our morning standup meetings between 9:00 AM and 9:30 AM Eastern Time, and also during the afternoon period between 2:00 PM and 3:00 PM Eastern Time. I am not sure if these times correspond to peak usage periods for the VPN infrastructure, but I thought it might be relevant information.

When the VPN disconnects, I see an error message that says 'The VPN connection was interrupted. Reconnecting...' followed by 'Connection attempt failed. Error code: 0x800B0109.' After about 30 seconds to a minute, the VPN usually reconnects automatically, but sometimes I need to manually disconnect and reconnect. On a few occasions, I had to restart the VPN client entirely.

I am currently using GlobalProtect version 6.2.1. I checked and it appears to be the latest version available in our Software Center. I also tried clearing the GlobalProtect cache and resetting the client configuration, but the issue persists.

This is impacting my productivity significantly because I lose access to internal resources like SharePoint, our internal wiki, and the development servers every time the VPN drops. Yesterday during an important client presentation via Teams, the VPN disconnected and I lost access to the demo environment for about two minutes, which was quite embarrassing.

I am in the Engineering department and my direct manager is David Park. My employee ID is EMP-45892 and my workstation asset tag is WS-NYC-12847. I am based in the New York office but working remotely from my home in New Jersey.

I have also spoken with three colleagues on my team who are experiencing similar issues:
- Jennifer Wu (jennifer.wu@contoso.com)
- Robert Kline (robert.kline@contoso.com)
- Aisha Patel (aisha.patel@contoso.com)

They have noticed the same pattern of disconnections at similar times of day, which makes me think this might be a broader infrastructure issue rather than something specific to my setup.

I would really appreciate it if someone could look into this. I am available for a screen-sharing session if that would be helpful for diagnosing the issue. My schedule is generally open between 10:00 AM and 4:00 PM Eastern Time, except for Tuesdays when I have back-to-back meetings.

Also, I forgot to mention - I recently traveled to our London office two weeks ago and the VPN worked fine there. The issue only occurs when I am at home. Although, as I mentioned, my colleagues who are also at their homes are experiencing the same thing, so it is probably not related to my specific home network.

One more thing - I ran a traceroute to our VPN gateway (vpn.contoso.com) and noticed some unusual latency spikes at hop 7, which appears to be somewhere in the ISP backbone. I saved the traceroute output to a text file and can share it if needed. The average latency to the VPN gateway was about 45ms, which seems normal, but there were occasional spikes to 200ms+ at that particular hop.

Thank you so much for your help with this. I know you have a lot on your plate and I really appreciate the support.

Best regards,
Michael Torres
Senior Software Engineer
Engineering Department
Contoso Financial Services
Phone: +1 (212) 555-0147
Email: michael.torres@contoso.com

---
This email and any attachments are confidential and intended solely for the use of the individual or entity to whom they are addressed. If you are not the intended recipient, please notify the sender immediately and delete this email.

subject: Outlook not syncing
description:
Hi IT team,

My Outlook hasn't been syncing emails since this morning. I can see emails on Outlook Web but the desktop app shows the last email from yesterday at 5:47 PM.

I've tried the following:

  • Restarted Outlook
  • Ran the scanpst.exe repair tool
  • Checked my internet connection — working fine
  • Cleared the Outlook cache folder at %localappdata%\Microsoft\Outlook

Nothing worked. My mailbox size is around 48GB if that matters.

I need this fixed ASAP — I’m expecting contract documents from a client today.

Karen Whitfield
Vice President, Institutional Trading
Contoso Financial Services
Phone: +1 (212) 555-0198
Email: karen.whitfield@contoso.com

CONFIDENTIALITY NOTICE: This email is intended only for the named recipient(s). If you received this in error, please notify the sender and delete all copies.

subject: Acceso VPN - problemas de conexión / VPN access issues
description:
Hola equipo de IT,

Tengo un problema con la conexión VPN desde la oficina de Singapore. El cliente GlobalProtect muestra "Gateway timeout" cuando intento conectar al gateway de Asia-Pacific.

(English translation for IT team: I'm having trouble connecting to the VPN from the Singapore office. GlobalProtect shows 'Gateway timeout' when connecting to the APAC gateway.)

He probado lo siguiente / I have tried the following:
- Reiniciar el cliente VPN / Restart the VPN client
- Cambiar al gateway de US-East / Switch to US-East gateway - this one works but latency is 300ms+
- Verificar credenciales / Verify credentials - login works on the web portal

Esto está afectando a todo el equipo de trading en Singapore (about 15 people). Necesitamos acceso a los servidores de New York para ejecutar trades y el gateway de backup tiene demasiada latencia.

Por favor, this is urgent — trading opens in 2 hours and we need low-latency access to the NYC trading systems.

Gracias / Thanks,
Carlos Mendez
Trading Desk - Singapore
